page: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance bundles 30000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in Passmark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall within the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks demonstrate strong throughput, while the Passmark memory score remains in the average tier.

In database and application-level benchmarks, the server shows strong performance for Redis SET operations, Passmark database operations, and static web serving throughput. Large-scale processing tasks, such as software compilation with Clang and image processing, also register in the strong tier. For large language model inference, the hardware delivers strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium models, though text generation speeds and large model processing drop to average levels.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the i4i.32xlarge is defined by its high density of local NVMe SSD storage paired with substantial memory and core counts. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for storage-intensive and I/O-heavy workloads, such as large-scale relational databases, NoSQL databases, and real-time data analytics, where local low-latency storage is critical. It is less optimal for workloads requiring leading single-core performance or dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It provides 128 vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz, 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 30000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Factual benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, and strong multi-core execution across stress-ng and Geekbench. However, single-core performance and memory scores align with average tiers. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, Redis throughput, and software compilation. Given its massive local storage capacity and balanced memory-to-core ratio, the i4i.32xlarge is suited for I/O-intensive databases, transactional workloads, and data-dense applications requiring high sequential and random read/write speeds without the need for GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 30000 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier Passmark CPU performance and strong database throughput.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 30000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining massive local NVMe capacity with high-core-count processing for demanding database and data-dense workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 30000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier Passmark CPU and encryption benchmark performance
Strong multi-core processing, database operations, and Redis throughput
Suited for large-scale databases, transactional systems, and I/O-heavy workloads
